description: Committed synthetic glTF/glb corpus covering the native fastgltf bridge, managed JSON layer, compatibility fallback, and performance baselines.

entries.description: Static .gltf scene with external buffer, external PNG image, TRS transforms, multiple UV/color sets, and deterministic material names.
entries.notes: Baseline native import regression asset for static scene routing, remap seeding, and compatibility fallback tests.

entries.description: Static .gltf scene with data URI buffer/image payloads, KHR_materials_unlit, and KHR_texture_transform texCoord override-only usage.
entries.notes: Covers data URI resource ownership, unlit material handling, and the supported KHR_texture_transform texCoord-override subset.

entries.description: Animated .gltf scene with skinning, default-scene selection, and translation/rotation animation channels.
entries.notes: Primary native regression asset for scene/default-scene selection, skinning, and transform animation coverage.

entries.description: Static .gltf scene with sparse colors, morph targets, default-scene selection, extras, and unknown material extension payloads.
entries.notes: Dedicated regression asset for sparse-accessor decode, morph target import, retained extras, and unknown extension payload preservation.

entries.description: Compact .glb scene with an embedded BIN chunk, buffer-view-backed PNG image, default-scene selection, and a matrix-authored mesh node.
entries.notes: Dedicated embedded-container fixture for GLB parsing, BIN chunk ownership, buffer-view image reads, and baked matrix transforms.

entries.description: Large synthetic .gltf scene with a shared grid mesh, many instance nodes, and external resources used for performance and parallel-scaling measurements.
entries.notes: Representative production-style workload for native vs Assimp wall-time, allocation, and parallel import comparison.

entries.description: Malformed .glb file with an out-of-bounds JSON chunk length.
entries.notes: Exercises deterministic GLB bounds validation before the native bridge is opened.
